changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to GDPR Article 25 by emphasizing data protection by design and by default. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but the audit logging function needed to specify the resource as 'apiserver' for clarity. All functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes security practices.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource specificity in function names, such as specifying 'apiserver' for audit logging.
title: Article 25 Data protection by design and by default
step1_reasoning: Article 25 of GDPR emphasizes data protection by design and by default, which can be mapped to several Kubernetes security practices. Ensuring least privilege access through RBAC aligns with minimizing data exposure. NetworkPolicies with default deny rules help in restricting unnecessary data flow. Encrypting secrets at rest ensures data protection by default. Enabling audit logging helps in monitoring and ensuring compliance with data protection requirements.
